summary: Tested new 3-tier role system (super_admin/admin/user) + new endpoints POST /api/admin/create-admin, PATCH /api/admin/users/{id}/role, DELETE /api/admin/users/{id}, PUT /api/properties/{id}, DELETE /api/properties/{id}. Also re-ran iteration_1 and iteration_2 regression suites. Total: 72/72 tests PASSED (100%). New role suite: 30/30. Regression: 42/42. Super admin seeded at startup works, admin-only endpoints correctly accept both admin and super_admin, super-admin-only endpoints correctly reject admin/user with 403. Delete property correctly removes pid from users' favorites arrays (verified end-to-end).

minor.issue: Allows promoting any user to 'super_admin' with no additional safeguard; a super_admin could accidentally create multiple super admins. Consider restricting role values to {'user','admin'} only, or requiring explicit confirmation for super_admin promotions.
When a user is deleted, their created bookings/inquiries (user_id=<deleted>) remain but become orphaned. Consider cascading cleanup or marking user_id as null.
No password strength validation; an admin can be created with password 'x' (single char). Same weakness exists on /auth/register. Consider a minimum-length Pydantic validator.

`db.users.update_many({}, {'$pull': ...})` runs unconditionally on EVERY user in the collection even when no user had favorited the property. For large user bases add a $match filter: `{'favorites': property_id}` to reduce write amplification.
Does not fetch target user first; if user_id does not exist, update_one silently returns matched_count=0 which is correctly translated to 404 - behavior ok, just noting the flow.

action_items: Optional: Restrict /api/admin/users/{id}/role role values to {'user','admin'} and require a separate flow to promote to super_admin.
Optional: Add minimum password strength (length >= 8) validator in UserRegister and AdminCreate Pydantic models.
Optional: Add $match filter `{'favorites': property_id}` on the $pull update in delete_property to avoid touching every user.
Optional: Consider cascading cleanup of orphaned bookings/inquiries when a user is deleted.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py now 747 lines - past the 700-line soft cap. Role helpers + admin user management endpoints (create-admin / update-role / delete-user) are a natural candidate to extract into /app/backend/admin_routes.py.

Duplicate role-check logic: many endpoints still inline `if token_data['role'] not in ('admin','super_admin')` instead of calling the new `require_admin(token_data)` helper (e.g., upload_image, create_property, update_property, delete_property, get_bookings, update_booking_status, get_inquiries, analytics/stats, analytics/traffic, get_all_users). Refactor to call `require_admin()` for DRY and consistency with require_super_admin.

seed_admin() at startup: good that super_admin+admin creation is idempotent (find first), but the logic is not atomic - under concurrent startup (multiple workers) both could insert duplicates since there is no unique index on users.email. Add a unique index on email.

No unique index on users.email in MongoDB - the duplicate-email check is purely at application layer and has a race window between find_one and insert_one. Should create `db.users.create_index('email', unique=True)` at startup.

PATCH /api/admin/users/{id}/role accepts `super_admin` as a valid role value - intentional per tests, but this means a super_admin can self-replicate the highest privilege without explicit guard. See minor issue above.

DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} uses find_one first (to check role) then delete_one, two DB calls. Could be a single findAndModify with a filter {role: {$ne: 'super_admin'}} for atomicity and one less round trip.
test_credentials: Super Admin: superadmin@rtgroups.info / SuperAdmin@2026 - working. Admin: admin@rtgroups.info / RTGroups@2026 - working. Test users registered dynamically with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: No manual seeding. All test data is created at runtime with TEST_ prefix: throwaway users, admins (via /api/admin/create-admin), and properties. Super admin / admin / sample properties are seeded automatically at FastAPI startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Role-based access control is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_roles.py (30 tests). Combined with backend_test.py (33) and test_upload.py (9) the suite is 72 tests, ~15s runtime. All pass against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. When adding new admin-only endpoints, add a test in TestSuperAdminAccess (test_roles.py) confirming super_admin can access too. When adding new super_admin-only endpoints, add negative tests for admin and regular user (403).
